message.content: An adversary may delete a cloud instance to evade detection and remove evidence of malicious activities. This technique can eliminate valuable forensic artifacts and other evidence of suspicious behavior, complicating incident response and investigation. Adversaries might create and later terminate cloud instances after achieving their objectives, as seen with groups like LAPSUS$, who have deleted target systems and resources to trigger organizational incident and crisis response processes. The potential impact includes loss of critical data, disruption of services, and increased difficulty in forensic analysis. Common exploitation methods involve leveraging cloud management APIs or administrative credentials to delete instances. Mitigation strategies include implementing strict access controls, enabling logging and monitoring of cloud management activities, and regularly backing up cloud instances to ensure recoverability.
